user_query:
We're the prime contractor on an IIJA-funded wastewater treatment plant upgrade in Franklin County, OH. Please prepare our week-ending certified payroll and a compliance package using the inputs provided.

Read these files:
- input/project.json — project context, WD metadata, week ending, apprenticeship ratio basis
- input/wd_rates.csv — DOL wage determination rates by classification (base and fringe per hour)
- input/employees.csv — weekly hours and employee-specific info (including any bona fide fringe plan credit per hour and apprentice details)

Deliverables (write all files under output/):
1) output/wh347_week1.csv — A WH-347 style weekly certified payroll export with a row per employee and these exact columns:
   - name,last4,classification,apprentice_flag,apprentice_pct,st_hours,ot_hours,base_rate,fringe_hourly_cash,gross_pay,deductions_total,net_pay,wd_number,week_ending
   Calculation rules:
   - Use the wage determination (WD) base rate and fringe per classification from input/wd_rates.csv.
   - If apprentice_flag is true, apply apprentice_pct (e.g., 0.70) to BOTH the base rate and fringe rate before further calculations.
   - Cash fringe owed per hour = max(0, required_fringe_per_hour − plan_credit_per_hour).
   - Overtime premium = 1.5 × base_rate for OT hours; no premium on fringe. Fringe is owed for all hours (ST and OT).
   - Gross pay = ST_hours × (base_rate + cash_fringe) + OT_hours × (1.5 × base_rate + cash_fringe).
   - Net pay = Gross pay − deductions_total from input/employees.csv.
   - Round all monetary fields (base_rate, fringe_hourly_cash, gross_pay, deductions_total, net_pay) to two decimals. Keep wd_number and week_ending from input/project.json.

2) output/wd_used.json — A summary of the wage determination used with fields:
   { "wd_number": string, "wd_type": string, "county": string, "state": string, "week_ending": string, "rates": [ {"classification": string, "base": number, "fringe": number}, ... ] }
   - The rates array must mirror the contents of input/wd_rates.csv.

3) output/wage_cost_summary.json — Totals and rollups with fields:
   {
     "total_st_hours": number,
     "total_ot_hours": number,
     "total_base_wages": number,
     "total_cash_fringe": number,
     "total_gross": number,
     "total_deductions": number,
     "total_net": number,
     "by_classification": { "<classification>": { "st_hours": number, "ot_hours": number, "base_wages": number, "cash_fringe": number, "gross": number }, ... }
   }
   Computation notes:
   - Base wages = ST_hours × base_rate + OT_hours × (1.5 × base_rate).
   - Cash fringe = (ST_hours + OT_hours) × cash_fringe.
   - Totals must reconcile exactly with the line items in output/wh347_week1.csv.
   - Round monetary values to two decimals.

4) output/compliance_checklist.md — A brief pass/fail checklist and corrective actions covering at least:
   - Correct classifications and WD rates applied (Pass/Fail)
   - Fringe benefits (plan credit vs. cash equivalent) (Pass/Fail)
   - Overtime premium calculation (Pass/Fail)
   - Apprenticeship registration and ratio compliance (Pass/Fail) — Use apprenticeship_ratio_limit and apprenticeship_ratio_basis from input/project.json to evaluate the Electrician craft. State the actual apprentice-to-journeyworker ratio for the week and whether it exceeds the limit, with a concrete corrective action if non-compliant.
   - WH-347 weekly Statement of Compliance included (Pass/Fail)
   - Wage determination posting and 3-year record retention (Pass/Fail)
   Include the WD number and week ending date at the top. Use explicit "Pass" or "Fail" labels and provide a short corrective action for each failed item.

5) output/risk_assessment.json — Calculate underpayment exposure if the Electrician apprentice in input/employees.csv were actually unregistered and therefore owed the full journeyworker Electrician rate and fringe for all hours worked. Use fields:
   {
     "employee": string,
     "craft": "Electrician",
     "apprentice_flag": true,
     "apprentice_pct": number,
     "hours": { "st": number, "ot": number },
     "paid_gross": number,
     "journeyworker_gross": number,
     "back_wages_due": number,
     "liquidated_damages": number,
     "total_exposure": number
   }
   Rules: journeyworker_gross = ST × (JW_base + JW_fringe) + OT × (1.5 × JW_base + JW_fringe). back_wages_due = journeyworker_gross − paid_gross. liquidated_damages = 100% of back_wages_due. total_exposure = back_wages_due + liquidated_damages. Round all monetary values to two decimals.

Please ensure all file paths match exactly and that calculations follow the rules above. If any craft in employees.csv is missing from input/wd_rates.csv, flag it in the checklist and note that an SF-1444 conformance request would be required.
